title: Stephen A. Smith responds to Jaylen Brown’s comments: ‘Be careful what you wish for’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephen A. Smith addressed Jaylen Brown's Twitch comments calling him 'the face of clickbait media' and urging him to retire.
Smith warned Brown about public criticisms, hinting at potential reporting on locker room dynamics and endorsement dealings.
Smith defended his previous comments on 'First Take', saying he was advising Brown against post-season declarations after a playoff loss.

title: FAA awarding Logan Airport $2.8 million for family-friendly upgrades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Logan Airport receives $2.8 million from the FAA for upgrading Kidports play areas.
Federal funding part of a $970 million initiative for family-friendly airport projects nationwide.
Logan's Kidports upgrades include new play structures located in Terminals A, B, and C.

title: Beverly mansion robbery suspect indicted on 10 charges
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Emajae Brown indicted on 10 charges, including armed burglary and kidnapping, in Beverly mansion robbery.
Brown allegedly stole nearly $8 million in valuables and attacked a housekeeper on March 28.
Investigators used license plate readers and surveillance cameras to identify Brown as the suspect.

title: Boston police commissioner questions previous sentence for alleged Cambridge shooter
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Commissioner Michael Cox criticized the lenient sentence given to alleged shooter Tyler Brown for a 2020 incident.
Brown received a five to six-year sentence for a 2020 shooting, despite prosecutors seeking a 10 to 12-year term.
Cox expressed concern over Brown's release, linking it to a recent shooting in Cambridge involving Brown.

title: Should Boston host the 2028 Democratic National Convention?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Hosting the 2028 DNC could generate $400 million in economic benefits for Boston, officials estimate.
Boston is a finalist alongside Atlanta, Chicago, Denver, and Philadelphia for the 2028 DNC hosting.
State and city officials, including Gov. Maura Healey, are showcasing Boston to DNC leaders during their selection tour.

title: Early thoughts on the Celtics’ offseason: Red flags, silver linings, and biggest needs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics face offseason decisions after losing 3-1 playoff series lead for first time in franchise history.
Key offseason needs include acquiring a scoring big man and a veteran two-guard to enhance depth.
Jayson Tatum's recurring playoff injuries and ineffective offensive style highlighted as major concerns.

title: 21-year-old woman charged after wrong-way crash with N.H. State Police cruiser
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cassandra Aldecoa, 21, charged after driving the wrong way and colliding with N.H. State Police trooper on Route 101.
Aldecoa faces felony charges including reckless conduct and second-degree assault; she also faces DUI-related misdemeanors.
Passenger Zachary Lapierre, 21, charged with disorderly conduct; both arraigned in Brentwood District Court, Aldecoa pleaded not guilty.

title: Franklin Park Zoo is getting two new gorillas as Little Joe heads to Pittsburgh
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Franklin Park Zoo is welcoming two new gorillas, Frankie from Pittsburgh and Moke from Washington, D.C., later this year.
Little Joe, a 33-year-old gorilla at Franklin Park Zoo, is relocating to Pittsburgh Zoo for breeding purposes.
The transition supports the Gorilla Species Survival Plan, promoting genetic diversity among western lowland gorillas in zoos.

title: Person medflighted after falling from zoo ride in Mendon
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Man medflighted after seizure and fall from Skyfari ride at Southwick Zoo in Mendon, Massachusetts.
Skyfari Sky Ride closed for safety inspection; ride uses only a lap bar without seatbelts or straps.
Southwick Zoo and state officials to review safety measures following incident; no other injuries reported.

title: New details emerge as Mass. McDonald’s employee accused of giving her ex-girlfriend contaminated fries
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Kaylie Santos charged with distributing food with a harmful substance after spitting in fries at McDonald's in Southbridge.
Santos allegedly gave contaminated fries to her ex-girlfriend during her shift as night manager, court records reveal.
Santos and affected individuals have parted ways with McDonald's, and she faces a no-trespass order and June 5 arraignment.
